title: [release-1.19] Bump golang.org/x/crypto/ssh
body:
Bump golang.org/x/crypto/ssh to v0.0.0-20220411220226-7b82a4e95df4
based on #3909 and addresses: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2073276
CVE-2022-27191 container-tools:3.0/podman: golang: crash in a golang.org/x/crypto/ssh server [rhel-8]

Signed-off-by: tomsweeneyredhat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/release-1.19/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This pulled in a lot more than main as it also required a bump in golang.org/x/sys.  I think that's OK.  @Luap99 does this make you twitch any?
I think this is ok, there is not much we can about indirect dependencies.

@cevich, I'm getting this error: 
```
[+0007s] make[1]: Leaving directory '/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/docs'
[+0007s] GO111MODULE=on go build -mod=vendor -ldflags '-X main.GitCommit=48af4e6 -X main.buildInfo=1651270277 -X main.cniVersion=v0.8.1 ' -gcflags "" -o bin/buildah -tags "seccomp    " ./cmd/buildah
[+0044s] # golang.org/x/net/http2
[+0044s] vendor/golang.org/x/net/http2/transport.go:417:45: undefined: os.ErrDeadlineExceeded
``

which from what I can see means Golang needs to be bumped up to  v1.17.3 or later.  Can you make that happen or point me in the right direction please?
go 1.16 is in fedora 35, we cannot merge code which only works with go 1.17 or newer.

> go 1.16 is in fedora 35, we cannot merge code which only works with go 1.17 or newer.

Aye, what Paul said, and worse: We cannot/shouldnot update VM images on a release-branch.  Eek! Look at the diff.! Oh man :confused: 

I'm straining to think of what options we **do** have here, and coming up empty :disappointed:   Best I got is to disable the failing tests, but jeeze, that's not an easy choice to swallow.  Any way to dial-back the vendoring to maintain 1.16 compatability?
We should probably just stop shipping on F35 at this point and only do CVE Fixes. perhaps disable previous OS, and move onto f37.

@rhatdan on `main` sure, no problem, but this is a release-branch.  So we preserve the CI environment in order to test backports to previous released versions.  So I think we actually need to use the old versions, in order to validate the new code didn't break previous/historical expectations.  Presumably `release-1.19` is going into a downstream product, is there a way we can verify if this change passes the downstream product tests?  I'm trying to think of alternatives to neutering testing of future backports to this branch.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
I'll close this.  The security team closed the BZ shortly after this failed, and I forgot to come back and clean this up.
